title: Worksheet: early-phase / single-arm studies
context: Single-arm and early-phase studies are frequently the source of advocacy hype: a response rate in a phase-I dose-expansion cohort is often reported as if it were comparative phase-III evidence. The RCT worksheet (task 007) is not appropriate for these designs. An original early-phase / single-arm appraisal worksheet gives advocates a structured way to interrogate what these studies can and cannot show, without copying licence-incompatible checklists.
objective: Author an original, CC-BY-licensed critical-appraisal worksheet for early-phase (phase I/II) and single-arm studies — authored from first principles, covering design, endpoints, response-rate interpretation, and the limits of inference — without copying or adapting any non-commercial or share-alike checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: Authored from methodological first principles; cites EQUATOR/CONSORT reporting extensions, Cochrane, and relevant FDA guidance as conceptual authority without reproducing copyrighted checklist text; passes the licence gate clean.

Covers: study design (dose-escalation, expansion cohort, single-arm phase II); primary endpoint (DLT, MTD, ORR); absence of a comparator and what that means for interpreting response rates; attrition and follow-up; patient population (eligibility criteria and generalisability); safety reporting; conflict of interest and funding.
Includes a 'what this study can and cannot show' summary section guiding advocates not to over-interpret single-arm results as comparative evidence.
Written in plain language; questions guide appraisal, not verdicts.
Printable and usable offline; clean Markdown renderable to PDF.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice.
Every methodological concept cited to sources.yaml with compatible allowedUse; passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI.
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as treatment guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): worksheet is usable and does not stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: appraisal questions are methodologically accurate for single-arm designs.
output: An original early-phase / single-arm critical-appraisal worksheet (content/worksheets/early-phase-appraisal.md) committed under CC-BY-4.0, provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review.
